basic_details.job_title: RPSC APO Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 371 Posts
basic_details.application_mode: Online only (via SSO Portal + RPSC website)
basic_details.short_information: RPSC is recruiting 371 Assistant Prosecution Officer posts with a Law degree qualification. The last date to apply is 07 July 2026.

important_dates.preliminary examination date: To be notified on RPSC website
important_dates.main examination date: To be notified after Preliminary Exam result

application_fee.details: Candidates are required to pay non-refundable Examination/Registration fees through online mode only. The One Time Registration (OTR) process is mandatory. Fee once paid will NOT be refunded under any circumstances. | Note: Candidates who registered via One Time Registration before 19.04.2023 and have already done OTR via SSO ID can directly apply using their OTR number/registration number on the portal.

age_limit.details: Candidates must be a minimum of 21 years and below 40 years as on 01-01-2027. The maximum age of 40 years is for Unreserved (UR) and EWS male candidates. Relaxation in upper age limit is applicable as per Rajasthan Government guidelines. | Important Notes on Age Relaxation: 1. The above age relaxations (points 01 to 11) are Non-Cumulative. Only one relaxation - the highest applicable - will be granted. Multiple relaxations cannot be added together. 2. Special relaxation under point 12 (PwBD) shall be applied additionally after point 01-11 relaxations are considered.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfil all eligibility conditions as on the date of eligibility (01-01-2027). Only Indian Citizens are eligible to apply. Candidates must mandatorily complete One Time Registration (OTR) on the SSO Portal before applying. | Note on Qualification: The last date for acquiring educational qualification is the last date of application, i.e., 07-07-2026. Candidates appearing in the final year of Law degree at the time of application are NOT eligible. Qualification must be complete as on the application closing date.
qualification.education_qualification: (1) Degree in Law (Professional) or integrated Law Course from a University established by law in India. (2) Working Knowledge of Hindi written in Devnagri Script and knowledge of Rajasthani dialects and social customs of Rajasthan.
qualification.domicile: Reserved category benefits applicable only to Rajasthan State domiciles. Candidates from other states belonging to SC/ST/OBC/MBC/EWS are treated as General category.

salary_details.pay_scale: Pay Matrix Level L-11 (Grade Pay Rs. 4,200/-); Fixed monthly pay as per State Government norms

selection_process: Feature Details
Type Objective (MCQ) - OMR based
Answer sheet BLUE BALL POINT PEN only
Options per question 5 options (1, 2, 3, 4, 5) - darken only one circle
Unattempted questions Must darken circle '5'. If none of the five circles is darkened, 1/3 mark will be deducted
Negative marking 1/3 mark deducted for each unattempted question where no circle is darkened
Disqualification Candidate who has not darkened any circle in more than 10% of questions shall be disqualified
Extra time 10 minutes beyond scheduled time provided to verify all bubbles are darkened
Purpose Qualifying only - marks not counted in final merit
Paper Subjects Maximum Marks Duration
Paper I Law 300 3 Hours
Paper II Language - 1. General Hindi 2. General English 50 + 50 = 100 2 Hours
Total 400 —
Selection for the post of Assistant Prosecution Officer will be based on (i) Preliminary Examination (Objective Type) and (ii) Main Examination (Written)

followed by document verification and medical fitness. The marks obtained in the Preliminary Examination shall NOT be counted towards the final merit/selection. A Wait List may be maintained as per RPSC norms.
The Preliminary Examination is an objective type examination. In the preliminary examination

70% weightage will be given to the Law paper (subjects prescribed in syllabus) and 30% weightage to test proficiency in Hindi and English language. The marks obtained in the preliminary examination shall NOT be counted towards the final selection.

Minimum qualifying marks for each paper: 40% (relaxed by 5% for SC/ST candidates). The level of Paper II (Language) shall be that of Senior Secondary (Class XII). It shall be compulsory to appear in each and every paper of the written test. The Law paper is designed to test practical knowledge in criminal law
procedure

and framing of charges in criminal cases. Ex-Servicemen and PwBD candidates are entitled to minimum qualifying marks relaxation as per Rajasthan Civil Services rules and Rajasthan Rights of Persons with Disabilities Rules
2018 respectively.

syllabus.details: The Indian Penal Code, 1860 The Indian Evidence Act, 1872 The Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973 The Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (BNS) The Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam, 2023 (BSA) The Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023 (BNSS) The Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989 The Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act, 2012 The Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act, 2015 The Probation of Offenders Act, 1958 The Arms Act, 1959 The Rajasthan Excise Act, 1950 The Rajasthan Public Examination (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, 1992 The Rajasthan Public Examination (Measures for Prevention of Unfair Means in Recruitment) Act, 2022 | General Hindi General English

how_to_apply: Candidates shall apply online only; no other mode of application will be accepted. Before applying, candidates must complete One Time Registration (OTR) on the SSO portal and read the detailed guidelines and instructions for applicants available on the RPSC website.
Candidates should have a valid email ID and active mobile number which should be kept active till declaration of results.
Visit the official RPSC website: https://rpsc.rajasthan.gov.in and read the complete notification and Instructions for Applicants before applying.
Click on the Apply Online link or log in to the SSO portal at https://sso.rajasthan.gov.in using your SSO ID to access the Recruitment Portal (G2C portal).

If you are a new user, complete One Time Registration (OTR) by providing your name, father's name (if available from Aadhaar), date of birth, gender, and Aadhaar card / Janaadhar card details. Upload a live self-photo and scan of OTR documents.
After completing OTR, log in from SSO portal → Citizen Apps (G2C) → Recruitment Portal and fill the online application form using your OTR number.

Important: No amendment to name, father's name, date of birth, or gender can be made in OTR Profile once created without Aadhaar/Janaadhar correction. Ensure all Aadhaar/Janaadhar details are corrected before completing OTR.
During online application, a Live Photo will be captured via OTP camera. Submit the form only after verifying the live photo is clear.
Candidates must scan and upload thumb impression (left hand) and signature along with the online application.
Candidates who wish to avail scribe facility must mandatorily select the scribe option in their online application form.
All educational qualification and experience details as at the application closing date must be clearly entered in the OTR/application form.
After submission, an Application Number will be generated. If no Application Number is received, it means the form has not been submitted successfully.
Candidates must take a printout of the finally submitted application form for future reference.
For any technical issues during online application, contact the Helpdesk number on the Recruitment Portal or email as indicated on the portal.

Call Letter (Admit Card) downloaded from RPSC website along with valid Photo ID proof (Aadhaar / PAN Card / Passport / Driving License / Voter ID / Bank Passbook with photo) in original and self-attested photocopy.
Caste Certificate in prescribed Government of India / Government of Rajasthan format (for SC/ST/OBC/MBC candidates).
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Certificate in prescribed format issued for the current year (for OBC NCL candidates).
Income and Asset Certificate issued by Competent Authority in prescribed format (for EWS candidates).
Disability Certificate in prescribed format issued by Competent Authority (for PwBD candidates).
Educational qualification certificates and marksheets (graduation / Law degree onwards).
Domicile certificate / Residence proof (for reserved category benefits applicable to Rajasthan domiciles).
Character Certificate from the last attended educational institution (at least "Good" mention required).
Police verification / antecedent certificate at the time of appointment.
Medical fitness certificate from a competent authority at the time of appointment.
NOC from current employer if already in Government / PSU service.
EWS / widowhood / divorce proof documents as applicable (court decree / death certificate).
Ex-Serviceman discharge certificate / NOC as applicable.
UIDAI / Unique Disability ID (U-D.I.D.) card, if available, must be produced before the Preliminary Examination.

Printout of the Online Application Form is NOT to be sent to RPSC. No Hard Copy of the Call Letter will be sent by post. Candidates must appear for the test at their own expense; RPSC will not pay or reimburse any such expenses.
important_instructions: Submitting false/incorrect information in the application form or failing to meet eligibility conditions is a punishable offence under Section 217 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (BNS). Such candidates may be debarred from appearing in the examination or disqualified during counselling/selection/appointment. All revisions, corrigenda, and updates will be hosted on the RPSC official website only - candidates should visit regularly. Candidates must not change their registered mobile number or email ID. Changes, if necessary, should be informed to RPSC at the earliest. Candidates must apply under the correct category. Applying under a wrong category will not entitle them to any category benefit; the application may be treated as General category. Candidates from other states belonging to SC/ST/OBC/MBC/EWS will be treated as General (UR) for this recruitment as reservation benefits are applicable only to Rajasthan domiciles. OBC candidates from Rajasthan belonging to the Creamy Layer must apply as General (UR). Only Non-Creamy Layer OBC candidates with a valid and recent certificate are eligible for OBC reservation. Category once filled in the online application form cannot be changed after the application submission deadline. Not more than one application should be submitted. In case of multiple applications, only the last valid completed application will be retained. PwBD candidates with temporary/likely-to-improve disabilities are not eligible for PwBD reservation benefits. Candidates already in Central / State / PSU Government service must produce a NOC from their employer at the time of appointment, or an NOC obtained before joining. Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification. No request for change of examination centre shall be entertained. Travel expenses will not be reimbursed by RPSC. The Commission reserves the right to cancel the selection process partly or entirely at any stage. Legal jurisdiction for any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be as per Hon'ble Rajasthan High Court, Jaipur / Jodhpur as applicable. Age relaxation provisions in Hindi language shall prevail over English translation in case of any discrepancy. Candidates who are provisionally selected will be given actual appointment only after document verification and medical fitness confirmation. Provisional admission to examination does not guarantee final appointment. Mobile phones, smartwatches, pagers, earphones, or any other electronic/communication devices are not allowed inside the examination premises.

all_official_links.label: Download RPSC APO Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for RPSC APO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to apply online for RPSC Assistant Prosecution Officer Recruitment 2026 is 07 July 2026. Candidates must complete the application process before midnight to avoid last-minute technical issues. It is advised to apply early and verify all details before final submission.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for RPSC APO 2026?
faq.answer: Applicants must possess a Bachelor’s Degree in Law (LLB Professional) from a recognized university in India. The qualification must be completed before the last date of application submission. Candidates who are in the final year of their law degree are not eligible to apply.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for RPSC Assistant Prosecution Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age required is 21 years and the maximum age is 40 years as on 01 January 2027. Age relaxation is provided to reserved categories such as SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and others as per Rajasthan government norms. Candidates must check category-wise relaxation carefully.

faq.question: Q4. How can I apply online for RPSC APO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply online through the official RPSC website or Rajasthan SSO portal. Before filling the application form, One Time Registration (OTR) is mandatory. Ensure all required documents are uploaded correctly and details match official records.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for RPSC APO 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee for General, BC, and MBC (Creamy Layer) candidates is Rs. 600. For SC, ST, OBC (Non-Creamy Layer), EWS, and PwBD candidates, the fee is Rs. 400. The fee must be paid online and is non-refundable under any circumstances.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for RPSC Assistant Prosecution Officer 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process consists of two stages: Preliminary Examination and Main Written Examination. The prelim exam is qualifying in nature and does not count toward final merit. Final selection is based on the marks obtained in the main examination.

faq.question: Q7. What subjects are included in the RPSC APO syllabus 2026?
faq.answer: The syllabus mainly focuses on criminal law subjects such as IPC, Evidence Act, CrPC, BNS, BNSS, and BSA. It also includes important acts like POCSO and SC/ST Act. Additionally, candidates are tested on General Hindi and English language skills.

faq.question: Q8. Is there negative marking in RPSC APO Preliminary Examination 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, there is negative marking in the preliminary examination as per RPSC rules. Candidates should answer questions carefully to avoid losing marks for incorrect answers. Accuracy along with speed is important to qualify this stage.

faq.question: Q9. What is the salary of RPSC Assistant Prosecution Officer?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will be placed under Pay Matrix Level L-11 with Grade Pay of Rs. 4200. During the probation period, fixed pay is provided as per Rajasthan government rules. After confirmation, additional allowances and benefits are applicable.

faq.question: Q10. Is One Time Registration (OTR) mandatory for RPSC APO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, One Time Registration (OTR) is compulsory for all candidates applying through the SSO portal. Without OTR, candidates cannot proceed with the application form. It is important to ensure that OTR details are accurate as they cannot be easily modified later.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for RPSC APO Recruitment 2026?
